# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains architectural description, project-scale metrics, interaction metrics, maintenance-cost observations, and observational case studies about a three-tier codified context infrastructure used with AI coding agents in a 108,000-line C# distributed system.

## Evidence Files

### file: evidence-01.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-013
evidence_id: evidence-01
kind: metric
citation: Vasilopoulos, Codified Context, Abstract
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2602.20478v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The reported system size was 108,000 lines of C# code.

## Source Excerpt

The abstract states the infrastructure was developed “during construction of a 108,000-line C# distributed system.”

## Note

The paper reports this as project scale context, not as a comparative benchmark.
```

### file: evidence-02.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-013
evidence_id: evidence-02
kind: result
citation: Vasilopoulos, Codified Context, Abstract
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2602.20478v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The proposed infrastructure has three components: a hot-memory constitution, 19 specialized agents, and a cold-memory knowledge base of 34 documents.

## Source Excerpt

The abstract describes “a three-component codified context infrastructure” consisting of “(1) a hot-memory constitution,” “(2) 19 specialized domain-expert agents,” and “(3) a cold-memory knowledge base of 34 on-demand specification documents.”

## Note

This is the paper’s main architectural decomposition.
```

### file: evidence-03.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-013
evidence_id: evidence-03
kind: method
citation: Vasilopoulos, Codified Context, Sec. 3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2602.20478v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The architecture assigns different loading strategies to each tier: constitution always loaded, specialist agents invoked per task, and knowledge-base documents retrieved on demand.

## Source Excerpt

Section 3 says the architecture has “three tiers, each with a distinct loading strategy and update frequency: hot memory (the constitution, always loaded), domain specialists (agents, invoked per task), and cold memory (the knowledge base, retrieved on demand).”

## Note

This is a direct statement of the system’s operating mechanism.
```

### file: evidence-04.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-013
evidence_id: evidence-04
kind: method
citation: Vasilopoulos, Codified Context, Sec. 3.1.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2602.20478v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The constitution uses trigger tables to route tasks to specialized agents based mainly on which files are being modified.

## Source Excerpt

Section 3.1.1 states: “The constitution embeds trigger tables that route tasks to the appropriate specialized agent (Tier 2) based on observable signals—primarily which files are being modified.”

## Note

This describes a concrete routing mechanism inside Tier 1.
```

### file: evidence-05.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-013
evidence_id: evidence-05
kind: claim-support
citation: Vasilopoulos, Codified Context, Sec. 3.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2602.20478v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The paper states that over half of each project agent specification was project-domain knowledge rather than behavioral instructions.

## Source Excerpt

In Section 3.2, “over half of each specification’s content is project-domain knowledge (codebase facts, formulas, code patterns, known failure modes) rather than behavioral instructions.”

## Note

The source presents this as an intentional design choice in agent construction.
```

### file: evidence-06.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-013
evidence_id: evidence-06
kind: method
citation: Vasilopoulos, Codified Context, Sec. 3.3.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2602.20478v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The knowledge base was served through an MCP server that exposed five search tools, including find_relevant_context(task) and suggest_agent(task).

## Source Excerpt

Section 3.3.1 says the knowledge base is served through an MCP server that “provides five search tools” and lists “find_relevant_context(task)” and “suggest_agent(task)” among them.

## Note

This is a concrete implementation detail for retrieval and routing support.
```

### file: evidence-07.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-013
evidence_id: evidence-07
kind: method
citation: Vasilopoulos, Codified Context, Sec. 3.3.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2602.20478v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
The current retrieval implementation used keyword substring matching rather than semantic retrieval.

## Source Excerpt

Section 3.3.1 states: “The current implementation uses keyword substring matching; semantic retrieval is discussed in Section 5.3.”

## Note

This bounds the retrieval mechanism and signals an explicit limitation relative to stronger retrieval approaches.
```

### file: evidence-08.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-013
evidence_id: evidence-08
kind: metric
citation: Vasilopoulos, Codified Context, Sec. 4.2 Table 3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2602.20478v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The project was developed across 283 sessions over 70 days of part-time work and 148 commits.

## Source Excerpt

Table 3 reports “Development period 70 days (part-time),” “Total commits 148,” and “Development sessions 283.”

## Note

These are project activity metrics, not controlled-performance measures.
```

### file: evidence-09.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-013
evidence_id: evidence-09
kind: metric
citation: Vasilopoulos, Codified Context, Sec. 4.2 Table 3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2602.20478v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The final context infrastructure totaled about 54 files and 26,200 lines, equal to 24.2% of code size.

## Source Excerpt

Table 3 lists “Total context 54 / ~26,200 / 24.2%” and breaks this into the constitution, specialized agents, and knowledge-base documents.

## Note

The paper later states this ratio is descriptive for this project, not a target.
```

### file: evidence-10.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-013
evidence_id: evidence-10
kind: claim-support
citation: Vasilopoulos, Codified Context, Sec. 4.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2602.20478v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
The author states that the 24.2% knowledge-to-code ratio should not be treated as a target or general finding.

## Source Excerpt

Section 4.2 says: “The knowledge-to-code ratio of 24.2% reflects this project’s complexity and domain, not a target or finding.”

## Note

This is an explicit caution against overgeneralizing one project’s ratio.
```

### file: evidence-11.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-013
evidence_id: evidence-11
kind: metric
citation: Vasilopoulos, Codified Context, Sec. 4.3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2602.20478v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The interaction dataset included 2,801 human prompts, 1,197 agent invocations, and 16,522 autonomous agent turns.

## Source Excerpt

Section 4.3 states: “The interaction dataset comprises 2,801 human prompts, 1,197 agent invocations, and 16,522 autonomous agent turns across these sessions.”

## Note

The source also reports 19,323 total interactions and about 9.9 human prompts per session.
```

### file: evidence-12.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-013
evidence_id: evidence-12
kind: metric
citation: Vasilopoulos, Codified Context, Sec. 4.3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2602.20478v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
About 13% of sessions used a structured plan-execute-review workflow, while about 87% were ad hoc.

## Source Excerpt

Section 4.3 says: “The orchestration protocols supported two modes: structured sessions (~13%) following a plan-execute-review workflow ... and ad-hoc sessions (~87%) involving direct implementation or debugging.”

## Note

This is a session-pattern distribution, not an effectiveness comparison.
```

### file: evidence-13.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-013
evidence_id: evidence-13
kind: incident
citation: Vasilopoulos, Codified Context, Sec. 4.4.1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2602.20478v1
confidence: medium
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
In the save-system case study, the specification was referenced in 74 sessions and 12 agent conversations, and the paper reports no save-related bugs across five later persistence features.

## Source Excerpt

Section 4.4.1 states that save-system.md was “the most-referenced specification in the project, appearing in 74 sessions and 12 agent conversations,” and that five subsequent persistence-related features “were implemented by agents with access to this specification,” after which “Coordination across 74 independent sessions over four weeks produced no save-related bugs.”

## Note

This is a case-study observation, not a controlled causal test.
```

### file: evidence-14.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-013
evidence_id: evidence-14
kind: incident
citation: Vasilopoulos, Codified Context, Sec. 4.4.4
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2602.20478v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
In the deterministic RNG debugging case, replacing a time-bucket hash input with a synchronized shot counter resolved the desynchronization bug.

## Source Excerpt

Section 4.4.4 says the agent concluded that “using time as an input to the combat RNG hash was fundamentally counterproductive” and that “Replacing the time-bucket parameter with a synchronized shot counter resolved the bug.”

## Note

The source presents this as an observed debugging outcome in one case study.
```

### file: evidence-15.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-013
evidence_id: evidence-15
kind: incident
citation: Vasilopoulos, Codified Context, Secs. 5.2 and 5.3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2602.20478v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
Specification staleness was reported as the primary failure mode, and the evaluation was limited to a single developer on a single project without controlled experiments.

## Source Excerpt

Section 5.2 states: “Specification staleness was the primary failure mode.” Section 5.3 states: “The architecture was developed by a single developer on a single project” and “The evaluation relies on observational case studies, not controlled experiments.”

## Note

These limits should remain attached to downstream use because they bound both reliability and generalizability.
---

Section 4.4.1 states that save-system.md was “the most-referenced specification in the project, appearing in 74 sessions and 12 agent conversations,” and that five subsequent persistence-related features “were implemented by agents with access to this specification,” after which “Coordination across 74 independent sessions over four weeks produced no save-related bugs.”

## Note

This is a case-study observation, not a controlled causal test.
```

In the deterministic RNG debugging case, replacing a time-bucket hash input with a synchronized shot counter resolved the desynchronization bug.

## Note

The source presents this as an observed debugging outcome in one case study.
```

---

Specification staleness was reported as the primary failure mode, and the evaluation was limited to a single developer on a single project without controlled experiments.

## Source Excerpt

Section 5.2 states: “Specification staleness was the primary failure mode.” Section 5.3 states: “The architecture was developed by a single developer on a single project” and “The evaluation relies on observational case studies, not controlled experiments.”

## Note

These limits should remain attached to downstream use because they bound both reliability and generalizability.

# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains architectural description, project-scale metrics, interaction metrics, maintenance-cost observations, and observational case studies about a three-tier codified context infrastructure used with AI coding agents in a 108,000-line C# distributed system.

## Evidence Files

### file: evidence-01.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-013
evidence_id: evidence-01
kind: metric

source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2602.20478v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The reported system size was 108,000 lines of C# code.

## Source Excerpt

The abstract states the infrastructure was developed “during construction of a 108,000-line C# distributed system.”

## Note

The paper reports this as project scale context, not as a comparative benchmark.
```

### file: evidence-02.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-013
evidence_id: evidence-02
kind: result
citation: Vasilopoulos, Codified Context, Abstract
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2602.20478v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The proposed infrastructure has three components: a hot-memory constitution, 19 specialized agents, and a cold-memory knowledge base of 34 documents.

## Source Excerpt

The abstract describes “a three-component codified context infrastructure” consisting of “(1) a hot-memory constitution,” “(2) 19 specialized domain-expert agents,” and “(3) a cold-memory knowledge base of 34 on-demand specification documents.”

## Note

This is the paper’s main architectural decomposition.
```

### file: evidence-03.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-013
evidence_id: evidence-03
kind: method
citation: Vasilopoulos, Codified Context, Sec. 3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/html/2602.20478v1
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: direct
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The architecture assigns different loading strategies to each tier: constitution always loaded, specialist agents invoked per task, and knowledge-base documents retrieved on demand.

## Source Excerpt

Section 3 says the architecture has “three tiers, each with a distinct loading strategy and update frequency: hot memory (the constitution, always loaded), domain specialists (agents, invoked per task), and cold memory (the knowledge base, retrieved on demand).”

## Note

This is a direct statement of the system’s operating mechanism.
```

Section 3.1.1 states: “The constitution embeds trigger tables that route tasks to the appropriate specialized agent (Tier 2) based on observable signals—primarily which files are being modified.”

## Note

This describes a concrete routing mechanism inside Tier 1.
```
